checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is root() computation deterministic across calls?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call root() multiple times on same trie with same callback. 2. Verify identical 32-byte output each time. 3. Check dictionary iteration order in _prepare_trie. 4. Test with various trie sizes and structures. 5. Verify no non-deterministic behavior from dict ordering.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0033, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Non-deterministic root makes consensus impossible.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does insert-then-delete restore original trie state?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Record original state root. 2. Set non-default value for new key. 3. Set default value for same key (delete). 4. Verify key not in trie._data. 5. Verify root() equals original root. 6. Test with SELFDESTRUCT and storage zeroing.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0034, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Permanent root change causes consensus failures.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is common_prefix_length computation bounded by O(N*L)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create many keys sharing long prefixes. 2. Measure common_prefix_length computation time. 3. Verify O(N*L) per recursive level. 4. Test for quadratic-or-worse complexity. 5. Check block processing timeout thresholds.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0035, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. Block processing may exceed timeout.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is state sync memory consumption bounded?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Send deeply nested branch nodes during sync. 2. Monitor memory allocation. 3. Check for stack overflow in recursive processing. 4. Test with all 16 children populated at each level. 5. Verify depth limits in sync implementation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0036, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Crashes prevent node from joining network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are all Fusaka types handled explicitly in encode_node?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all Fusaka/Osaka types in state trie. 2. Verify isinstance checks are exhaustive. 3. Test each type reaches correct encoding path. 4. Submit transactions triggering each type. 5. Compare encoding with previous_trie.encode_node output.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0037, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Wrong encoding path causes consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is type-safe equality used in trie_set default comparison?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft value with pathological equality behavior. 2. Test if value incorrectly compares equal to trie.default. 3. Check for type coercion in equality check. 4. Verify no implicit conversion (e.g., b'' vs None, 0 vs False). 5. Test with custom __eq__ implementations if possible.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0038, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Silent deletion causes unauthorized fund loss.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does encode_node produce non-empty output for all valid values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test encode_node with all valid state value types. 2. Verify output != b'' for each type. 3. Test Fusaka-introduced types specifically. 4. Check boundary conditions between valid and invalid values. 5. Verify _prepare_trie assertion catches empty output.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0039, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Empty encoding crashes all nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does trie_set maintain non-default values invariant?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. After each trie_set, verify all keys map to non-default values. 2. Check for default values persisting in _data. 3. Test edge cases in delete path. 4. Verify state root consistency. 5. Test with rapid set/delete cycles.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0040, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Default values in trie alter state root.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is encode_node isinstance dispatch order correct for subclasses?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify any subclass relationships in encoded types. 2. Verify more specific checks come before general checks. 3. Test with new Fusaka transaction types subclassing existing types. 4. Compare isinstance ordering across client implementations. 5. Check for type misrouting causing wrong encoding.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0041, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Wrong dispatch produces different state roots.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is compact encoding leaf/extension distinction preserved in roundtrips?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Encode various leaf and extension nodes. 2. Decode and verify is_leaf flag preserved exactly. 3. Craft Merkle proofs exploiting leaf/extension ambiguity. 4. Test proof verification rejects forged proofs. 5. Check roundtrip consistency for all path lengths.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0042, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Leaf/extension confusion enables state proof forgery.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is root_node type correct at RLP encoding boundary?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create trie producing root_node at RLP size boundary. 2. Verify root_node is Bytes (hash) when RLP >= 32 bytes. 3. Check Root() constructor receives 32-byte hash. 4. Test edge cases at exact boundary. 5. Compare type handling across implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0043, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Incorrect type causes validation failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is get_storage_root callback deterministic?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call get_storage_root multiple times for same account. 2. Verify identical Root value returned each time. 3. Test during concurrent storage modifications. 4. Check for caching issues causing inconsistency. 5. Test with Fusaka storage layout changes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0046, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Non-determinism causes network-wide consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is copy_trie safe against mutable value aliasing?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify all trie value objects are immutable/frozen. 2. Check for new Fusaka mutable types in trie. 3. Test modification through copy affecting original. 4. Check during re-org handling and speculative execution. 5. Verify no cross-block state corruption possible.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0047, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Aliasing causes non-deterministic state.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is Osaka account encoding field order correct?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify encode_account includes nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash in order. 2. Compare encoding with Osaka specification. 3. Test cross-client encoding consistency. 4. Submit account-modifying transactions. 5. Verify identical state roots across implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0048, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Wrong order causes immediate consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Falsification Check: Can empty nibble list compact encoding be made incorrect?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test nibble_list_to_compact([], is_leaf=False) produces 0x00. 2. Test nibble_list_to_compact([], is_leaf=True) produces 0x20. 3. Verify single-byte output with correct parity flag. 4. Test keys matching branch levels exactly. 5. Attempt to produce incorrect encoding at empty boundary.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0049. Empty nibble lists occur at branch boundaries.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is cross-client state root consensus maintained for all Osaka state transitions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Execute identical transactions on multiple client implementations. 2. Compare computed state roots after each block. 3. Test all Fusaka-specific transaction types. 4. Verify consensus on edge cases (empty trie, boundary conditions). 5. Check for any discrepancy indicating consensus failure.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0050, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-007. Fundamental safety property - chain split is most severe outcome.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction gasLimit > 16777216 rejected at txpool admission?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction via P2P with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Verify the transaction is rejected by the txpool. 3. Confirm the transaction does not appear in pending transactions. 4. Test with gasLimit values: 16777216 (accept), 16777217 (reject), 2^32 (reject).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0001, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is GAS_CAP constant exactly 16777216 (2^24)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Inspect the GAS_CAP constant in the client source code. 2. Verify it equals exactly 16777216 (0x1000000). 3. Compare the constant value across all major client implementations (geth, nethermind, besu, erigon). 4. Verify the constant cannot be modified at runtime.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0002, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is tx gas cap independent from block gas limit?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test network with block_gas_limit > 16777216 (e.g., 30M). 2. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 20000000 (above tx cap but below block limit). 3. Verify the transaction is rejected at txpool. 4. Verify if somehow included in a block, block validation rejects it.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0003, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation iterate ALL transactions for gas cap check?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft a block with N valid transactions followed by 1 oversized transaction. 2. Submit via P2P and verify block is rejected. 3. Craft a block with 1 oversized transaction followed by N valid transactions. 4. Verify block is still rejected (no early-exit optimization). 5. Test with oversized transaction at positions 1, N/2, and N.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0004, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: INV-005 (SG-002, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is entire block rejected if ANY transaction exceeds gas cap?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block with 99 valid transactions and 1 with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Submit the block and verify complete rejection. 3. Verify no partial acceptance (block should not be processed at all). 4. Check that no state changes occur from the valid transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0005, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: INV-006 (SG-002, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-Condition: All transactions in valid blocks have gasLimit <= 16777216?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Query recent validated blocks. 2. For each block, verify all transactions have gasLimit <= 16777216. 3. Attempt to inject a block with an oversized transaction and verify it never reaches the execution engine. 4. Monitor execution engine logs for any transaction with gasLimit > cap.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0006, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Post-condition for block validation

checklist_item.title: Verify Defense-in-Depth: Are both txpool and block validation enforcing gas cap independently?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Disable or bypass txpool validation (if possible in test mode). 2. Verify block validation still catches oversized transactions. 3. Disable or bypass block validation (if possible in test mode). 4. Verify txpool validation still catches oversized transactions. 5. Confirm both layers operate independently.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0007, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, Invariant: INV-007 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is cap value 16777216 identical at txpool and block validation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Audit source code for both txpool and block validation gas cap constants. 2. Verify both reference the same constant or have identical values. 3. Search for any hardcoded values that might diverge. 4. Test boundary: submit tx with gasLimit = 16777216 and verify consistent behavior at both layers.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0008, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-008 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Can rejected transactions leak into block building via race conditions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit oversized transactions while block building is in progress. 2. Use concurrent requests to trigger potential TOCTOU race. 3. Monitor block builder for any inclusion of rejected transactions. 4. Test with high-frequency submission near block boundaries. 5. Review synchronization mechanisms in txpool-to-builder pipeline.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0009, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Invariant: INV-009 (SG-003, EIP-7825)
